title: mentha lapponica subsp. parietariifolia
intro: Mentha lapponica subsp. parietariifolia, also known as Arctic water mint or Lapland mint, is a subspecies of perennial herb belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). It is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia, and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Mentha lapponica subsp. parietariifolia include its ability to alleviate digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and combat respiratory infections, making it a popular ingredient in traditional herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, making it a valuable addition to any herbal medicine cabinet.
